title: Protocol JSON Schema + Safety/Provenance sub-schemas + canonical data model
context: lab-protocols-open is schema-first: no protocol may be authored until the JSON Schema contract exists, because schema validity is the foundation for the validator, linter, renderer, and interoperability with Bioschemas/schema.org. The schema must capture steps, materials (CAS/hazardClass/SDS refs), equipment, safety, ethics flags, QC, troubleshooting, versioning, and provenance, with a Safety sub-schema that auto-escalates riskTier to high on any hazard/biohazard/animal/human-material flag. This is M0's foundational technical deliverable.
objective: Design and publish the Protocol JSON Schema (validated with ajv) including Safety and Provenance sub-schemas, a canonical data model, safety auto-escalation rules, and a documented crosswalk to Bioschemas LabProtocol / schema.org fields.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON Schema validates with ajv; covers steps, materials (casNumber, hazardClass, sdsRef), equipment, safety, ethics flags, qc, troubleshooting, provenance, dualUseScreen, review, semver version + changelogRef.
Safety auto-escalation encoded and documented: any hazard/biohazard/animal/human-material flag implies riskTier:high; authors cannot downgrade.
Every step and every safety item has a required provenance reference field.
Maps to Bioschemas LabProtocol / schema.org fields; interop crosswalk documented.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass for committed schema/tooling; commit DCO signed-off.
output: A committed Protocol JSON Schema (packages/schema or equivalent) with Safety and Provenance sub-schemas, ajv-validated, safety auto-escalation documented, and a Bioschemas/schema.org crosswalk document; CI green.
